item.title: Algorithms and optimization for power systems reliability
item.description:
Understanding the fundamental interplay between network topology and failure propagation in power systems using graph theory and spectral methods.

See below for the list of related publications and preprints.

item.title: MCMC methods for rare events and non-convex supports
item.description:
Developing novel sampling methods, with applications to rare event sampling and stochastic optimization.

See below for the list of related publications and preprints.

item.title: Rare events in stochastic networks
item.description:
Unveiling how correlated noise and coupled network dynamics can increase the likelihood of rare events, such as blackouts in power systems with a large renewable penetration.

See below for the list of related publications and preprints.

item.title: Learning in power networks
item.description:
Spectral clustering, adaptive network topology, and safe learning for power systems applications.

See below for the list of related publications and preprints.

item.title: Random-access networks
item.description:
Quantifying throughput and delay performance of wireless networks protocols using Markov chains and heavy-traffic limits.

See below for the list of related publications and preprints.

item.title: Asymptotic behavior of interacting particle systems
item.description:
Studying hitting and mixing times for interacting particle systems with stochastic evolutions and finite volume.

See below for the list of related publications and preprints.

item.title: An MILP-based approach to tree partitioning with minimal power flow disruption and generator coherency constraints
Refining bridge-block decompositions through two-stage and recursive tree partitioning
A Metropolis-class sampler for targets with non-convex support
Hopping between distant basins
A Spectral Representation of Power Systems with Applications to Adaptive Grid Partitioning and Cascading Failure Localization
Line Failure Localization of Power Networks. Part II: Cut Set Outages
Line Failure Localization of Power Networks. Part I: Non-cut outages
An Integrated Approach for Failure Mitigation & Localization in Power Systems
Optimization of stochastic lossy transport networks and applications to power grids
Mitigating Cascading Failures via Local Responses
Large fluctuations in locational marginal prices
Weighted Dyck paths for nonstationary queues
Less is More: Real-time Failure Localization in Power Systems
Tunneling behavior of Ising and Potts models in the low-temperature regime
Tunneling of the hard-core model on finite triangular lattices
Temporal starvation in multi-channel CSMA networks: An analytical framework
Frequency violations from random disturbances: an MCMC approach
Failure Localization in Power Systems via Tree Partitions
Emergent failures and cascades in power grids: A statistical physics perspective
item.description:
Related news items

  • How Correlated Weather Fluctuations Take Down Power Grids (APS Synopsis)
  • CWI Researchers Make Power Grids More Reliable With Mathematics (CWI release)
  • New Model Shows Where to Improve Power Grids (Resnick press release)

item.title: Low-temperature behavior of the multicomponent Widom–Rowlison model on finite square lattices
Line failure probability bounds for power grids
Minimizing heat loss in DC networks using batteries
Example Project
item.description:

item.title: On the Interactions Between Multiple Overlapping WLANs Using Channel Bonding
Hitting Time Asymptotics for Hard-Core Interactions on Grids
Spatio-temporal dynamics of random-access networks: An interacting particle approach (PhD thesis)
Slow Transitions and Starvation in Dense Random-Access Networks
Throughput analysis in CSMA/CA networks using continuous time Markov networks: A tutorial
Delay performance in random-access grid networks
Mixing properties of CSMA networks on partite graphs
